You are working in /home/uzer/baz on the baz-marketing-site repository (Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind). The BattleCardForm component is at components/marketing/BattleCardForm.tsx, competitor data is in content/competitors.ts, leads API is at app/api/leads/route.ts, and the existing PDF parsing lib is at lib/data/book-ingest.ts.

Your task: Build a battle card PDF generation and delivery system:
1. Create an API route at app/api/battle-cards/route.ts that accepts POST { email, competitor, name, company }
2. When a valid request comes in, generate a PDF battle card summarizing the competitor vs BAZventures comparison. You can use a simple HTML-to-PDF approach or the existing pdf-parse patterns in lib/data/book-ingest.ts.
3. Store the lead in /api/leads with source="battle_card" (the form already does this, but ensure the API route also creates/updates the lead)
4. Send the PDF to the user's email. Since we can't use real email in dev, make this pluggable: create lib/battle-cards.ts with a sendBattleCard(email, competitor, pdfBuffer) function that logs to console in dev and can be swapped for Resend/SendGrid in production
5. Return { ok: true, message: "Battle card sent" } on success
6. Add rate limiting using the existing lib/rate-limit.ts

Constraints:
- Do NOT add new npm packages unless strictly necessary
- Reuse existing patterns (actions, rate-limit, db, etc.)
- The PDF should include: BAZventures logo wordmark, competitor name, pricing comparison table, top 3 limits, top 3 BAZventures strengths, and a CTA to book a call
- PDF generation can use a simple approach: generate an HTML string, then use a lightweight conversion or just return the HTML as a "battle card preview" if PDF libs are too heavy

Return a summary of all files changed and what you implemented.
Let me start by exploring the codebase to understand the existing patterns, files, and structure before implementing the battle card PDF generation system.

Instructions from: /home/uzer/baz/AGENTS.md
# BAZventures — Next.js + Supabase + Vercel

**Brand:** BAZventures · **Product:** the Hub · **Founder:** Brahim ZERBOUT  
**Design system source of truth:** `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md` (read it first).

## Project Setup
- GitHub repo: `baz-marketing-site` (remote: `origin`)
- Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Realtime, Storage, Edge Functions)
- Vercel (Analytics, Speed Insights, OG Images, Cron, Edge Runtime)
- CI/CD via GitHub Actions (test → deploy preview → deploy production)

## Design — Black & White Color System
- **B&W by default.** Color is a single expert-editable layer: `app/color-layer.css` (imported **last** in `app/layout.tsx` → always wins). Set `--seed-hue`/`--seed-sat` there to recolor the whole site (e.g. cyan `187/90%`, gold `42/85%`). One file, two numbers.
- **Dark mode only** — `ThemeProvider` forces `dark` (`forcedTheme="dark"`, `enableSystem={false}`). Background/text are seed-driven neutrals (currently greyscale).
- **4-layer CSS stack:** `globals.css` → `aether-theme.css` → `aether-monochrome.css` → `color-layer.css` (last wins). Full map in `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md`.
- **Fonts:** Outfit (display), Poppins (body), JetBrains Mono (code/metrics).
- **Square corners everywhere** (`* { border-radius: 0 }`); `rounded-full` only for pills/badges.
- **Tokens, not hexes:** never hardcode color in components — use semantic Tailwind classes (`text-foreground`, `bg-background`, `text-brand`, `bg-accent`, `border-border`) → tokens → `color-layer.css`.

## Brand Assets (B&W)
- **Favicon:** `public/favicon.svg` — B&W mark (dark squircle, light "B").
- **Logo (live):** a **text wordmark** rendered in `components/layout/Header.tsx` + `Footer.tsx` — a `bg-foreground` "B" square + "BAZventures" in Outfit. B&W, token-driven, auto-recolors via `--brand` when the seed is set.
- **Legacy logo SVGs** in `public/logo/` (`baz-mark*.svg`, `baz-wordmark*.svg`) are retained for the `/brandbook` reference page but are **not** used by the live header/footer.
- **OG images:** `/og`, `/stance-og`, `/case-studies-og`, `/methodology-og` — neutral palette, "BAZventures" wordmark.
- **No gold/accent color** anywhere on the public site until the expert sets the seed in `color-layer.css`.

## Key Scripts
- `npm run dev` — local dev (port 3000)
- `npm run build` — production build
- `npm test` — Playwright E2E tests
- `npm run supabase:types` — regenerate TS types from Supabase
- `npm run supabase:migrate` — push migrations to Supabase
- `npm run deploy` — deploy to Vercel

## Supabase
- Project: `uyqgmdrzyapbbvmaumvk.supabase.co`
- Migrations: `supabase/migrations/`
- Edge Functions: `supabase/functions/`
- Types: `lib/database.types.ts`
- Client: `lib/supabase-client.ts` (SSR + Admin + Anonymous)
- Realtime: `lib/realtime.ts` (client-side hooks)

## Vercel
- Project: `baz-marketing-site-y6bq` (team: brahimamirzerbout-9700s-projects)
- Analytics + Speed Insights in layout
- Dynamic OG images at `/og`
- Cron jobs: sitemap (daily), audit (weekly)
- Edge runtime: `/api/services`, `/api/health`

## CI/CD (GitHub Actions)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
- PR → typecheck + lint + deploy preview + E2E tests
- Push to main → deploy production + Supabase migrations + health check

</system-reminder>

77:   if (!/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(email))
78:     return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "invalid_email" }, { status: 400 });
79: 
80:   const db = getDb();
81:   const leadId = id("l");
82:   const source = (body.source || "marketing_site").toString().slice(0, 64);
83:   const service = (body.service || "").toString().slice(0, 96);
84:   db.prepare(
85:     `INSERT INTO leads (id, name, email, company, website, phone, budget, message, source, service)
86:               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
87:   ).run(
88:     leadId,
89:     name,
90:     email,
91:     body.company?.toString().slice(0, 200),
92:     body.website?.toString().slice(0, 500),
93:     body.phone?.toString().slice(0, 64),
94:     body.budget?.toString().slice(0, 64),
95:     message,
96:     source,
97:     service,
98:   );
99:   audit(null, "lead.create", leadId, { source, service });
100: 
101:   // Always run deterministic scoring — it's free and works without LLM.
102:   // Layer LLM scoring on top if configured.
103:   const { scoreLead } = await import("@/lib/scoring");
104:   const baseScore = scoreLead({
105:     message,
106:     budget: body.budget as string | undefined,
107:     company: body.company as string | undefined,
108:     website: body.website as string | undefined,
109:     source,
110:     service,
111:     demoCompleted: !!body.demoCompleted,
112:     agentRuns: typeof body.agentRuns === "number" ? body.agentRuns : undefined,
113:     timeOnPageSec: typeof body.timeOnPageSec === "number" ? body.timeOnPageSec : undefined,
114:     scrollDepth: typeof body.scrollDepth === "number" ? body.scrollDepth : undefined,
115:     isRepeat: !!body.isRepeat,
116:   });
117:   db.prepare("UPDATE leads SET score = ?, intent = ? WHERE id = ?").run(
118:     baseScore.score,
119:     baseScore.intent,
120:     leadId,
121:   );
122:   audit(null, "lead.score", leadId, {
123:     score: baseScore.score,
124:     intent: baseScore.intent,
125:     reasons: baseScore.reasons,
126:   });
127: 
128:   // Fire-and-forget: layer LLM scoring on top if available.
129:   if (process.env.LEAD_SCORING !== "off") {
130:     scoreLeadInBackground(leadId, message).catch(() => {});
131:   }
132: 
133:   return NextResponse.json({
134:     ok: true,
135:     id: leadId,
136:     score: baseScore.score,
137:     intent: baseScore.intent,
138:     action: baseScore.recommendedAction,
139:   });
140: }
141: 
142: async function scoreLeadInBackground(leadId: string, message: string) {
143:   const { complete } = await import("@/lib/llm");
144:   const result = await complete({
145:     prompt: `Refine the lead score (currently 0-100 baseline) based on this message. Return JSON {"adjust": <int between -20 and +20>, "intent": "<buy_now|researching|comparison_shopping|tire_kicker>"}.\\n\\nMessage:\\n${message.slice(0, 2000)}`,
146:     system: `You are BAZ LeadGen Agent. Adjust the deterministic lead score by -20 to +20 based on subtleties. Return strict JSON only.`,
147:     maxTokens: 200,
148:     temperature: 0.2,
149:   });
150:   if (!result.ok || !result.text) return;
151:   try {
152:     const parsed = JSON.parse(result.text.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/)?.[0] ?? "{}");
153:     const adjust = Math.max(-20, Math.min(20, parseInt(parsed.adjust, 10) || 0));
154:     const intent = String(parsed.intent || "").slice(0, 32);
155:     if (adjust !== 0 || intent) {
156:       const db = getDb();
157:       const row = db.prepare("SELECT score FROM leads WHERE id = ?").get(leadId) as
158:         { score: number } | undefined;
159:       const base = row?.score ?? 0;
160:       const final = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, base + adjust));
161:       db.prepare("UPDATE leads SET score = ?, intent = ? WHERE id = ?").run(
162:         final,
163:         intent || undefined,
164:         leadId,
165:       );
166:     }
167:   } catch {
168:     // ignore parse errors
169:   }
170: }
171: 
172: /**
173:  * PATCH /api/leads?id=X — update status / score / owner. Auth required.
174:  */
175: export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {
176:   const { user } = await readSessionFromCookies();
177:   if (!user) return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "unauthenticated" }, { status: 401 });
178:   const url = new URL(req.url);
179:   const leadId = url.searchParams.get("id");
180:   if (!leadId) return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "missing_id" }, { status: 400 });
181: 
182:   const body = await req.json().catch(() => ({}));
183:   const db = getDb();
184:   const sets: string[] = [];
185:   const args: (string | number)[] = [];
186:   for (const field of ["status", "score", "owner"]) {
187:     if (field in body) {
188:       sets.push(`${field} = ?`);
189:       args.push(body[field]);
190:     }
191:   }
192:   if (sets.length === 0)
193:     return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "no_fields" }, { status: 400 });
194:   sets.push("updated_at = ?");
195:   args.push(Date.now());
196:   args.push(leadId);
197:   db.prepare(`UPDATE leads SET ${sets.join(", ")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...args);
198:   audit(user.id, "lead.update", leadId, body);
199:   return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });
200: }

21: // ── Types ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22: 
23: export interface BookMeta {
24:   title: string;
25:   author: string;
26:   tags: string[];
27:   source: "upload" | "url" | "manual";
28:   format: "pdf" | "epub" | "txt" | "md" | "url";

31: export interface BookChunk {
32:   id: string;
33:   bookId: string;
34:   content: string;
35:   chapter: string;
36:   chapterIndex: number;
37:   chunkIndex: number;
38:   tokenCount: number;

45:   bookId: string;
46:   title: string;
47:   totalChunks: number;
48:   totalTokens: number;
49:   format: string;
50:   status: "ok" | "partial" | "error";

55: // ── Configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────
56: 
57: const CHUNK_SIZE_TOKENS = 500;
58: const CHUNK_OVERLAP_TOKENS = 50;
59: const MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH = 2_000_000; // 2MB text limit per book
60: 
61: // Rough token estimate: 1 token ≈ 4 chars for English
62: function estimateTokens(text: string): number {
63:   return Math.ceil(text.length / 4);
64: }
65: 
66: // ── Text Extraction ─────────────────────────────────────────
67: 
68: /**
69:  * Extract text from a PDF buffer.
70:  * Requires `pdf-parse` package.
71:  */
72: async function extractPdf(buffer: Buffer): Promise<{ text: string; pages: number }> {
73:   try {
74:     const pdfParse = await import("pdf-parse");
75:     const data = await pdfParse.default(buffer);
76:     return { text: data.text, pages: data.numpages };
77:   } catch {
78:     throw new Error(
79:       "pdf-parse not installed. Run: npm install pdf-parse @types/pdf-parse",
80:     );
81:   }
82: }
83: 
84: /**
85:  * Extract text from an EPUB buffer.
86:  * Requires `epub2` package.
87:  */
88: async function extractEpub(buffer: Buffer): Promise<{ text: string; chapters: string[] }> {
89:   // EPUB extraction: epub2 has TS compatibility issues.
90:   // For now, attempt ZIP-based extraction (most EPUBs are ZIP archives with HTML).
91:   // Falls back to raw binary scan if JSZip is unavailable.
92:   try {
93:     let JSZip: any = null;
94:     try { const mod = await import("jszip"); JSZip = mod.default || mod; } catch { /* not available */ }
95:     
96:     if (JSZip) {
97:       const zip = await JSZip.loadAsync(buffer);
98:       const htmlFiles: string[] = [];
99:       const chapterNames: string[] = [];
100:       
101:       for (const [name, file] of Object.entries(zip.files)) {
102:         if (/\.(html|xhtml|htm)$/i.test(name) && !name.includes("toc") && !name.includes("nav")) {
103:           try {
104:             const content = await file.async("string");
105:             const plainText = content
106:               .replace(/<[^>]*>/g, " ")
107:               .replace(/&\w+;/g, " ")
108:               .replace(/\s+/g, " ")
109:               .trim();
110:             if (plainText.length > 50) {
111:               htmlFiles.push(plainText);
112:               const titleMatch = content.match(/<title[^>]*>([^<]+)<\/title>/i);
113:               chapterNames.push(titleMatch?.[1] || `Chapter ${chapterNames.length + 1}`);
114:             }
115:           } catch { /* skip unreadable files */ }
116:         }
117:       }
118:       
119:       if (htmlFiles.length > 0) {
120:         return { text: htmlFiles.join("\n\n---\n\n"), chapters: chapterNames };
121:       }
122:     }
123:     
124:     throw new Error("EPUB parsing requires jszip. Install with: npm install jszip");
125:   } catch (err) {
126:     throw new Error(
127:       `EPUB parsing failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`,
128:     );
129:   }
130: }
131: function extractText(content: string): { text: string; chapters: string[] } {
132:   // Detect markdown headings as chapter boundaries
133:   const lines = content.split("\n");
134:   const chapters: string[] = [];
135:   let currentChapter = "Introduction";
136:   let chapterTexts: { name: string; text: string }[] = [{ name: currentChapter, text: "" }];
137: 
138:   for (const line of lines) {
139:     const headingMatch = line.match(/^#{1,3}\s+(.+)/);
140:     if (headingMatch) {
141:       currentChapter = headingMatch[1].trim();
142:       chapters.push(currentChapter);
143:       chapterTexts.push({ name: currentChapter, text: "" });
144:     } else {
145:       chapterTexts[chapterTexts.length - 1].text += line + "\n";
146:     }
147:   }
148: 
149:   if (chapters.length === 0) {
150:     chapters.push("Full Text");
151:     chapterTexts = [{ name: "Full Text", text: content }];
152:   }
153: 
154:   const text = chapterTexts
155:     .map((c) => `\n\n--- ${c.name} ---\n\n${c.text}`)
156:     .join("");
157: 
158:   return { text, chapters };
159: }
160: 
161: /**
162:  * Extract text from a web URL.
163:  */
164: async function extractUrl(url: string): Promise<{ text: string }> {
165:   const res = await fetch(url);
166:   if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`Failed to fetch ${url}: ${res.status}`);
167: 
168:   const html = await res.text();
169: 
170:   // Simple HTML → text extraction (no dependency needed)
171:   const text = html
172:     // Remove scripts and styles
173:     .replace(/<script[\s\S]*?<\/script>/gi, "")
174:     .replace(/<style[\s\S]*?<\/style>/gi, "")
175:     // Remove tags
176:     .replace(/<[^>]*>/g, " ")
177:     // Decode entities
178:     .replace(/ /g, " ")
179:     .replace(/&/g, "&")
180:     .replace(/</g, "<")
181:     .replace(/>/g, ">")
182:     .replace(/"/g, '"')
183:     // Normalize whitespace
184:     .replace(/\s+/g, " ")
185:     .trim();
186: 
187:   return { text };
188: }
189: 
190: // ── Chunking ────────────────────────────────────────────────
191: 
192: /**
193:  * Split text into overlapping chunks of approximately `chunkSize` tokens.
194:  * Preserves paragraph boundaries when possible.
195:  */
196: export function chunkText(
197:   text: string,
198:   chunkSize: number = CHUNK_SIZE_TOKENS,
199:   overlap: number = CHUNK_OVERLAP_TOKENS,
200: ): Array<{
201:   content: string;
202:   chunkIndex: number;
203:   tokenCount: number;
204:   startOffset: number;
205: }> {
206:   const paragraphs = text.split(/\n\s*\n/).filter((p) => p.trim().length > 0);
207:   const chunks: Array<{
208:     content: string;
209:     chunkIndex: number;
210:     tokenCount: number;
211:     startOffset: number;
212:   }> = [];
213: 
214:   let currentChunk = "";
215:   let currentTokens = 0;
216:   let offset = 0;
217: 
218:   for (const para of paragraphs) {
219:     const paraTokens = estimateTokens(para);
220: 
221:     // If adding this paragraph exceeds chunk size and we have content, save the chunk
222:     if (currentTokens + paraTokens > chunkSize && currentChunk.length > 0) {
223:       chunks.push({
224:         content: currentChunk.trim(),
225:         chunkIndex: chunks.length,
226:         tokenCount: currentTokens,
227:         startOffset: offset,
228:       });
229: 
230:       // Overlap: keep the last `overlap` tokens worth of text
231:       const overlapText = getOverlapText(currentChunk, overlap);
232:       currentChunk = overlapText + "\n\n" + para;
233:       currentTokens = estimateTokens(currentChunk);
234:       offset += currentChunk.length - overlapText.length;
235:     } else {
236:       currentChunk += (currentChunk ? "\n\n" : "") + para;
237:       currentTokens += paraTokens;
238:     }
239:   }
240: 
241:   // Don't forget the last chunk
242:   if (currentChunk.trim().length > 0) {
243:     chunks.push({
244:       content: currentChunk.trim(),
245:       chunkIndex: chunks.length,
246:       tokenCount: estimateTokens(currentChunk),
247:       startOffset: offset,
248:     });
249:   }
250: 
251:   return chunks;
252: }
253: 
254: function getOverlapText(text: string, overlapTokens: number): string {
255:   const overlapChars = overlapTokens * 4; // rough estimate
256:   if (text.length <= overlapChars) return text;
257:   const slice = text.slice(-overlapChars);
258:   // Start from the first paragraph boundary in the overlap
259:   const paraBreak = slice.indexOf("\n\n");
260:   return paraBreak >= 0 ? slice.slice(paraBreak + 2) : slice;
261: }
262: 
263: /**
264:  * Parse chunked text into chapter-aware chunks.
265:  * Each chunk gets the chapter title from the nearest preceding "---" marker.
266:  */
267: export function assignChapters(
268:   chunks: Array<{ content: string; chunkIndex: number; tokenCount: number; startOffset: number }>,
269: ): Array<{
270:   content: string;
271:   chunkIndex: number;
272:   tokenCount: number;
273:   startOffset: number;
274:   chapter: string;
275:   chapterIndex: number;
276: }> {
277:   // Extract chapter markers from the full text
278:   let currentChapter = "Introduction";
279:   let chapterIndex = 0;
280: 
281:   return chunks.map((chunk) => {
282:     const chapterMarkers = chunk.content.match(/---\s+(.+?)\s+---/g);
283:     if (chapterMarkers) {
284:       // Use the last chapter marker in this chunk
285:       const lastMarker = chapterMarkers[chapterMarkers.length - 1];
286:       const chapterName = lastMarker.replace(/---\s+/, "").replace(/\s+---/, "");
287:       currentChapter = chapterName;
288:     }
289: 
290:     // Remove chapter markers from content for cleaner embedding
291:     const cleanContent = chunk.content
292:       .replace(/---\s+.+?\s+---/g, "")
293:       .trim();
294: 
295:     const result = {
296:       content: cleanContent,
297:       chunkIndex: chunk.chunkIndex,
298:       tokenCount: estimateTokens(cleanContent),
299:       startOffset: chunk.startOffset,
300:       chapter: currentChapter,
301:       chapterIndex,
302:     };
303: 
304:     // Increment chapter index if we see a new chapter marker
305:     if (chapterMarkers && chapterMarkers.length > 0) {
306:       chapterIndex++;
307:     }
308: 
309:     return result;
310:   });
311: }
312: 
313: // ── Main Ingestion Pipeline ─────────────────────────────────
314: 
315: /**
316:  * Ingest a book from a file buffer or URL.
317:  * Returns chunked and embedded data ready for storage.
318:  */
319: export async function ingestBook(
320:   input: {
321:     buffer?: Buffer;
322:     url?: string;
323:     text?: string;
324:     meta: BookMeta;
325:   },
326:   embedderConfig?: EmbedderConfig,
327: ): Promise<IngestResult> {
328:   const bookId = `book_${crypto.randomBytes(6).toString("hex")}`;
329:   const errors: string[] = [];
330:   let rawText = "";
331:   let format = input.meta.format;
332: 
333:   // Step 1: Extract text
334:   try {
335:     if (input.text) {
336:       format = "txt";
337:       rawText = input.text;
338:     } else if (input.url) {
339:       format = "url";
340:       const result = await extractUrl(input.url);
341:       rawText = result.text;
342:     } else if (input.buffer) {
343:       if (format === "pdf") {
344:         const result = await extractPdf(input.buffer);
345:         rawText = result.text;
346:       } else if (format === "epub") {
347:         const result = await extractEpub(input.buffer);
348:         rawText = result.text;
349:       } else {
350:         // Try as plain text
351:         rawText = input.buffer.toString("utf-8");
352:         format = "txt";
353:       }
354:     } else {
355:       throw new Error("Must provide buffer, url, or text");
356:     }
357:   } catch (err) {
358:     return {
359:       bookId,
360:       title: input.meta.title,
361:       totalChunks: 0,
362:       totalTokens: 0,
363:       format,
364:       status: "error",
365:       errors: [err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)],
366:     };
367:   }
368: 
369:   // Validate text length
370:   if (rawText.length > MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH) {
371:     rawText = rawText.slice(0, MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH);
372:     errors.push(`Text truncated to ${MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH} characters`);
373:   }
374: 
375:   if (rawText.trim().length < 100) {
376:     return {
377:       bookId,
378:       title: input.meta.title,
379:       totalChunks: 0,
380:       totalTokens: 0,
381:       format,
382:       status: "error",
383:       errors: ["Extracted text too short (less than 100 characters)"],
384:     };
385:   }
386: 
387:   // Step 2: Chunk text
388:   const rawChunks = chunkText(rawText);
389:   const chunks = assignChapters(rawChunks);
390: 
391:   if (chunks.length === 0) {
392:     return {
393:       bookId,
394:       title: input.meta.title,
395:       totalChunks: 0,
396:       totalTokens: 0,
397:       format,
398:       status: "error",
399:       errors: ["No chunks produced from text"],
400:     };
401:   }
402: 
403:   // Step 3: Generate embeddings
404:   const config = embedderConfig ?? getEmbedderConfig();
405:   const texts = chunks.map((c) => c.content);
406:   let embeddings: number[][];
407: 
408:   try {
409:     embeddings = await embedBatch(texts, config);
410:   } catch (err) {
411:     // Embedding failed — return chunks with empty embeddings so they can still be saved
412:     const emptyChunks = chunks.map((c, i) => ({
413:       id: `${bookId}_chunk_${i}`,
414:       bookId,
415:       content: c.content,
416:       chapter: c.chapter,
417:       chapterIndex: c.chapterIndex,
418:       chunkIndex: c.chunkIndex,
419:       tokenCount: c.tokenCount,
420:       startOffset: c.startOffset,
421:       endOffset: c.startOffset + c.content.length,
422:       embedding: [] as number[],
423:     }));
424:     return {
425:       bookId,
426:       title: input.meta.title,
427:       totalChunks: emptyChunks.length,
428:       totalTokens: emptyChunks.reduce((sum, c) => sum + c.tokenCount, 0),
429:       format,
430:       status: "partial",
431:       errors: [`Embedding failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`],
432:       chunks: emptyChunks,
433:     };
434:   }
435: 
436:   if (embeddings.length !== chunks.length) {
437:     errors.push(
438:       `Embedding count mismatch: ${embeddings.length} embeddings for ${chunks.length} chunks`,
439:     );
440:   }
441: 
442:   // Step 4: Build final chunks with embeddings
443:   const bookChunks: BookChunk[] = chunks.map((chunk, i) => ({
444:     id: `${bookId}_chunk_${i}`,
445:     bookId,
446:     content: chunk.content,
447:     chapter: chunk.chapter,
448:     chapterIndex: chunk.chapterIndex,
449:     chunkIndex: chunk.chunkIndex,
450:     tokenCount: chunk.tokenCount,
451:     startOffset: chunk.startOffset,
452:     endOffset: chunk.startOffset + chunk.content.length,
453:     embedding: embeddings[i] || [],
454:   }));
455: 
456:   // Step 5: Store (caller must save — we return the data)
457:   const totalTokens = bookChunks.reduce((sum, c) => sum + c.tokenCount, 0);
458: 
459:   return {
460:     bookId,
461:     title: input.meta.title,
462:     totalChunks: bookChunks.length,
463:     totalTokens,
464:     format,
465:     status: errors.length > 0 ? "partial" : "ok",
466:     errors,
467:     chunks: bookChunks,
468:   };
469: }
470: 
471: /**
472:  * Ingest a book and return the chunks (without embedding — for testing).
473:  * The caller can embed later.
474:  */
475: export function ingestBookDryRun(
476:   text: string,
477:   meta: BookMeta,
478: ): { bookId: string; chunks: Array<Omit<BookChunk, "embedding">>; totalTokens: number } {
479:   const bookId = `book_${crypto.randomBytes(6).toString("hex")}`;
480:   const rawChunks = chunkText(text);
481:   const chunks = assignChapters(rawChunks);
482: 
483:   const totalTokens = chunks.reduce((sum, c) => sum + c.tokenCount, 0);
484: 
485:   return {
486:     bookId,
487:     chunks: chunks.map((c) => ({
488:       id: `${bookId}_chunk_${c.chunkIndex}`,
489:       bookId,
490:       content: c.content,
491:       chapter: c.chapter,
492:       chapterIndex: c.chapterIndex,
493:       chunkIndex: c.chunkIndex,
494:       tokenCount: c.tokenCount,
495:       startOffset: c.startOffset,
496:       endOffset: c.startOffset + c.content.length,
497:     })),
498:     totalTokens,
499:   };
500: }

// @ts-nocheck
/**
 * BAZventures — Book ingestion pipeline
 *
 * Takes PDF/EPUB/TXT/MD files and converts them into searchable chunks
 * with embeddings for RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation).
 *
 * Flow:
 *   Raw file → Extract text → Chunk (500 tokens, 50 overlap) → Embed → Store
 *
 * Supported formats:
 *   - PDF (via pdf-parse)
 *   - EPUB (via epub2)
 *   - TXT / MD (native)

1: /**
2:  * BAZ — Rate limiter (pluggable store).
3:  *
4:  * Defaults to an in-memory store (single-process). On Vercel serverless,
5:  * swap the backend by calling `setRateLimitStore(new VercelKvRateLimitStore())`
6:  * from a startup path (e.g., a Next.js instrumentation hook or a module
7:  * init guarded by `process.env.KV_REST_API_URL`).
8:  *
9:  * Consumer API (`rateLimit`, `rateLimitHeaders`, `rateLimitedResponse`)
10:  * is unchanged.
11:  */
12: 
13: import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
14: import { getRateLimitStore, type Bucket } from "./rate-limit-store";
15: 
16: interface RateLimitOpts {
17:   key: string;
18:   limit: number;
19:   windowMs: number;
20:   userId?: string;
21: }
22: 
23: export function rateLimit(
24:   req: Request,
25:   opts: RateLimitOpts,
26: ): { ok: true; remaining: number; resetAt: number } | { ok: false; retryAfter: number } {
27:   if (process.env.NODE_ENV === "production") {
28:     const store = getRateLimitStore();
29:     if (store instanceof (require("./rate-limit-store").MemoryRateLimitStore || Object)) {
30:       // Surface the per-instance warning once per cold start on Vercel.
31:       if (!(globalThis as any).__baz_rate_limit_warned) {
32:         (globalThis as any).__baz_rate_limit_warned = true;
33:         console.error(
34:           "[baz:rate-limit] in-memory store is per-instance only. Set Vercel KV / Upstash Redis before public launch.",
35:         );
36:       }
37:     }
38:   }
39: 
40:   const ip =
41:     req.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() ??
42:     req.headers.get("x-real-ip") ??
43:     "unknown";
44:   const k = `${opts.key}:${opts.userId ?? ip}`;
45:   const now = Date.now();
46:   const store = getRateLimitStore();
47:   let existing = store.get(k);
48: 
49:   if (!existing || existing.resetAt < now) {
50:     const next: Bucket = { count: 1, resetAt: now + opts.windowMs };
51:     store.set(k, next);
52:     store.prune(now);
53:     return { ok: true, remaining: opts.limit - 1, resetAt: now + opts.windowMs };
54:   }
55: 
56:   if (existing.count >= opts.limit) {
57:     return { ok: false, retryAfter: Math.ceil((existing.resetAt - now) / 1000) };
58:   }
59: 
60:   const next: Bucket = { ...existing, count: existing.count + 1 };
61:   store.set(k, next);
62:   return { ok: true, remaining: opts.limit - next.count, resetAt: next.resetAt };
63: }
64: 
65: export function rateLimitHeaders(
66:   result: { ok: true; remaining: number; resetAt: number } | { ok: false; retryAfter: number },
67: ): HeadersInit {
68:   const headers: Record<string, string> = {};
69:   if (result.ok) {
70:     headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"] = String(result.remaining);
71:     headers["X-RateLimit-Reset"] = String(Math.ceil(result.resetAt / 1000));
72:   } else {
73:     headers["Retry-After"] = String(result.retryAfter);
74:   }
75:   return headers;
76: }
77: 
78: export function rateLimitedResponse(result: { retryAfter: number }): NextResponse {
79:   return NextResponse.json(

85:         "X-RateLimit-Reset": String(Math.ceil((Date.now() + result.retryAfter * 1000) / 1000)),
86:       },
87:     },
88:   );
89: }

476: 
477:       // CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS <name> ( ... )
478:       const createMatch = stmt.match(/^CREATE\s+TABLE\s+IF\s+NOT\s+EXISTS\s+(\w+)/i);
479:       if (createMatch) {
480:         ensureTable(createMatch[1]);
481:         continue;
482:       }
483: 
484:       // CREATE INDEX ... (no-op in memory)
485:       if (/^CREATE\s+INDEX/i.test(stmt)) continue;
486: 
487:       // ALTER TABLE <name> ADD COLUMN <col> <type> (no-op — rows are dynamic objects)
488:       if (/^ALTER\s+TABLE/i.test(stmt)) continue;
489:     }
490:   }
491: 
492:   function prepare(sql: string) {
493:     return {
494:       run(...args: unknown[]): unknown {
495:         const { action, table, columns, values, where, pkValue } = parseSql(sql, args);
496:         ensureTable(table);
497: 
498:         if (action === "INSERT") {
499:           const row: Record<string, unknown> = {};
500:           columns.forEach((col, i) => { row[col] = values[i]; });
501:           // Auto-increment for INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
502:           tables[table].push(row);
503:           if (pkValue != null) pkIndex[table][String(pkValue)] = tables[table].length - 1;
504:           return { changes: 1, lastInsertRowid: tables[table].length };
505:         }
506: 
507:         if (action === "UPDATE") {
508:           let changes = 0;
509:           for (const row of tables[table]) {
510:             if (matchesWhere(row, where, args)) {
511:               columns.forEach((col, i) => { row[col] = values[i]; });
512:               changes++;
513:             }
514:           }
515:           return { changes };
516:         }
517: 
518:         if (action === "DELETE") {
519:           const before = tables[table].length;
520:           tables[table] = tables[table].filter((row) => !matchesWhere(row, where, args));
521:           return { changes: before - tables[table].length };
522:         }
523: 
524:         return { changes: 0 };
525:       },
526: 
527:       get(...args: unknown[]): unknown {
528:         const { action, table, where } = parseSql(sql, args);
529:         ensureTable(table);
530: 
531:         if (action === "SELECT") {
532:           // SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM <table> [WHERE ...]
533:           const countMatch = sql.match(/SELECT\s+COUNT\(\*\)\s+AS\s+(\w+)/i);
534:           if (countMatch) {
535:             const rows = tables[table].filter((row) => matchesWhere(row, where, args));
536:             return { [countMatch[1]]: rows.length };
537:           }
538:           // SELECT * FROM <table> WHERE col = ? [ORDER BY ... LIMIT ?]
539:           const rows = tables[table].filter((row) => matchesWhere(row, where, args));
540:           return rows[0] ?? undefined;
541:         }
542: 
543:         // PRAGMA table_info(<table>)
544:         const pragmaMatch = sql.match(/PRAGMA\s+table_info\((\w+)\)/i);
545:         if (pragmaMatch) {
546:           ensureTable(pragmaMatch[1]);
547:           const sample = tables[pragmaMatch[1]][0];
548:           if (!sample) return [];
549:           return Object.keys(sample).map((name) => ({ name }));
550:         }
551: 
552:         return undefined;
553:       },
554: 
555:       all(...args: unknown[]): unknown[] {
556:         const { action, table, where } = parseSql(sql, args);
557:         ensureTable(table);
558: 
559:         if (action === "SELECT") {
560:           let rows = tables[table].filter((row) => matchesWhere(row, where, args));
561:           // ORDER BY <col> DESC/ASC
562:           const orderMatch = sql.match(/ORDER\s+BY\s+(\w+)\s+(DESC|ASC)/i);
563:           if (orderMatch) {
564:             const col = orderMatch[1];
565:             const dir = orderMatch[2].toUpperCase();
566:             rows = [...rows].sort((a, b) => {
567:               const av = a[col] as number | string;
568:               const bv = b[col] as number | string;
569:               if (av < bv) return dir === "DESC" ? 1 : -1;
570:               if (av > bv) return dir === "DESC" ? -1 : 1;
571:               return 0;
572:             });
573:           }
574:           // LIMIT ?
575:           const limitMatch = sql.match(/LIMIT\s+\?/i);
576:           if (limitMatch) {
577:             const limitIdx = args.length - 1;
578:             const limit = Number(args[limitIdx]);
579:             if (limit > 0) rows = rows.slice(0, limit);
580:           }
581:           return rows;
582:         }
583: 
584:         // SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS n FROM <table> GROUP BY status
585:         const groupMatch = sql.match(/SELECT\s+(\w+),\s*COUNT\(\*\)\s+AS\s+\w+\s+FROM\s+(\w+)\s+GROUP\s+BY/i);
586:         if (groupMatch) {
587:           const col = groupMatch[1];
588:           ensureTable(groupMatch[2]);
589:           const groups: Record<string, number> = {};
590:           for (const row of tables[groupMatch[2]]) {
591:             const key = String(row[col] ?? "");
592:             groups[key] = (groups[key] ?? 0) + 1;
593:           }
594:           return Object.entries(groups).map(([k, n]) => ({ status: k, n }));
595:         }
596: 
597:         return [];
598:       },
599:     };
600:   }
601: 
602:   function pragma(str: string): unknown {
603:     // no-op for in-memory
604:     return undefined;
605:   }
606: 
607:   // Tiny SQL parser — extracts action, table, columns, values, where clause
608:   function parseSql(sql: string, args: unknown[]): {
609:     action: string;
610:     table: string;
611:     columns: string[];
612:     values: unknown[];
613:     where: { col: string; op: string; argIdx: number }[] | null;
614:     pkValue: unknown;
615:   } {
616:     const trimmed = sql.trim();
617:     const upper = trimmed.toUpperCase();
618: 
619:     // INSERT INTO <table> (cols...) VALUES (?, ?, ...)
620:     const insertMatch = trimmed.match(/^INSERT\s+INTO\s+(\w+)\s*\(([^)]+)\)\s*VALUES\s*\(([^)]+)\)/i);
621:     if (insertMatch) {
622:       const table = insertMatch[1];
623:       const columns = insertMatch[2].split(",").map((s) => s.trim());
624:       const placeholders = insertMatch[3].split(",").map((s) => s.trim());
625:       const values = placeholders.map((_, i) => args[i]);
626:       // Find PK for index
627:       let pkValue: unknown = undefined;
628:       const pkCol = columns[0]; // convention: first col is PK
629:       pkValue = values[0];
630:       return { action: "INSERT", table, columns, values, where: null, pkValue };
631:     }
632: 
633:     // UPDATE <table> SET col = ?, col2 = ? WHERE col = ?
634:     const updateMatch = trimmed.match(/^UPDATE\s+(\w+)\s+SET\s+(.+?)\s+WHERE\s+(.+)/i);
635:     if (updateMatch) {
636:       const table = updateMatch[1];
637:       const setPart = updateMatch[2];
638:       const wherePart = updateMatch[3];
639:       const setCols = setPart.split(",").map((s) => s.trim().split("=")[0].trim());
640:       // Count SET placeholders + WHERE placeholders
641:       const setPlaceholders = (setPart.match(/\?/g) || []).length;
642:       const setValues = args.slice(0, setPlaceholders);
643:       const whereArgs = args.slice(setPlaceholders);
644:       const where = parseWhereClause(wherePart, 0);
645:       // Shift where arg indices
646:       const adjustedWhere = where?.map((w) => ({ ...w, argIdx: w.argIdx + setPlaceholders })) ?? null;
647:       return { action: "UPDATE", table, columns: setCols, values: setValues, where: adjustedWhere, pkValue: undefined };
648:     }
649: 
650:     // DELETE FROM <table> WHERE col = ?
651:     const deleteMatch = trimmed.match(/^DELETE\s+FROM\s+(\w+)\s+WHERE\s+(.+)/i);
652:     if (deleteMatch) {
653:       const where = parseWhereClause(deleteMatch[2], 0);
654:       return { action: "DELETE", table: deleteMatch[1], columns: [], values: [], where, pkValue: undefined };
655:     }
656: 
657:     // SELECT ... FROM <table> [WHERE ...] [ORDER BY ...] [LIMIT ?]
658:     const selectMatch = trimmed.match(/^SELECT\s+.+?\s+FROM\s+(\w+)/i);
659:     if (selectMatch) {
660:       const table = selectMatch[1];
661:       const whereMatch = trimmed.match(/WHERE\s+(.+?)(?:\s+ORDER\s+BY|\s+LIMIT|\s+GROUP\s+BY|$)/i);
662:       const where = whereMatch ? parseWhereClause(whereMatch[1].trim(), 0) : null;
663:       return { action: "SELECT", table, columns: [], values: [], where, pkValue: undefined };
664:     }
665: 
666:     return { action: "UNKNOWN", table: "", columns: [], values: [], where: null, pkValue: undefined };
667:   }
668: 
669:   function parseWhereClause(clause: string, offset: number): { col: string; op: string; argIdx: number }[] | null {
670:     // Parse "col = ?" or "col = ? AND col2 = ?" etc.
671:     const conditions: { col: string; op: string; argIdx: number }[] = [];
672:     const parts = clause.split(/\s+(?:AND|OR)\s+/i);
673:     let argIdx = offset;
674:     for (const part of parts) {
675:       const m = part.match(/(\w+)\s*(=|!=|>|<|>=|<=|LIKE)\s*\?/i);
676:       if (m) {
677:         conditions.push({ col: m[1], op: m[2], argIdx });
678:         argIdx++;
679:       }
680:     }
681:     return conditions.length ? conditions : null;
682:   }
683: 
684:   function matchesWhere(row: Record<string, unknown>, where: { col: string; op: string; argIdx: number }[] | null, args: unknown[]): boolean {
685:     if (!where || where.length === 0) return true;
686:     return where.every((w) => {
687:       const val = row[w.col];
688:       const arg = args[w.argIdx];
689:       switch (w.op.toUpperCase()) {
690:         case "=": return val == arg;
691:         case "!=": return val != arg;
692:         case ">": return Number(val) > Number(arg);
693:         case "<": return Number(val) < Number(arg);
694:         case ">=": return Number(val) >= Number(arg);
695:         case "<=": return Number(val) <= Number(arg);
696:         case "LIKE":
697:           const pattern = String(arg).replace(/%/g, ".*").replace(/_/g, ".");
698:           return new RegExp(`^${pattern}$`, "i").test(String(val ?? ""));
699:         default: return false;
700:       }
701:     });
702:   }
703: 
704:   return { prepare, exec, pragma } as SqliteDb;
705: }

3: 
4: export interface StoredLead {

19: 
20: interface LeadStatusEntry {
21:   status: LeadStatus;
22:   updatedAt: string;
23:   note?: string;
24: }
25: 
26: /**
27:  * Status is stored as a sidecar file (`data/leads.status.json`) so the
28:  * append-only leads.jsonl stays pure and the migration cost stays zero.
29:  *
30:  * For up to a few thousand leads this in-memory read+write is fine.
31:  * Beyond that, switch the status sidecar to SQLite (same file as the
32:  * leads file, single atomic write).
33:  */
34: const DATA_DIR = path.join(process.cwd(), "data");
35: const LEADS_FILE = path.join(DATA_DIR, "leads.jsonl");
36: const STATUS_FILE = path.join(DATA_DIR, "leads.status.json");
37: 
38: interface StatusMap {
39:   [leadId: string]: LeadStatusEntry;
40: }
41: 
42: async function readStatus(): Promise<StatusMap> {
43:   try {
44:     const raw = await fs.readFile(STATUS_FILE, "utf8");
45:     return JSON.parse(raw) as StatusMap;
46:   } catch {
47:     return {};
48:   }
49: }
50: 
51: async function writeStatus(map: StatusMap): Promise<void> {
52:   await fs.mkdir(DATA_DIR, { recursive: true });
53:   // Write to a tmp file then rename — atomic on POSIX, prevents partial
54:   // writes from corrupting the status sidecar.
55:   const tmp = STATUS_FILE + ".tmp";
56:   await fs.writeFile(tmp, JSON.stringify(map, null, 2), "utf8");
57:   await fs.rename(tmp, STATUS_FILE);
58: }
59: 
60: export async function readLeads(): Promise<StoredLead[]> {
61:   let raw: string;
62:   try {
63:     raw = await fs.readFile(LEADS_FILE, "utf8");
64:   } catch {
65:     return [];
66:   }
67:   const lines = raw.split("\n").filter(Boolean);
68:   const all = lines
69:     .map((l) => {
70:       try {
71:         return JSON.parse(l) as StoredLead;
72:       } catch {
73:         return null;
74:       }
75:     })
76:     .filter((x): x is StoredLead => x !== null);
77: 
78:   // Newest first
79:   all.sort((a, b) => b.receivedAt.localeCompare(a.receivedAt));
80:   return all;
81: }
82: 
83: export async function readLeadsWithStatus(): Promise<
84:   Array<StoredLead & { status: LeadStatus; updatedAt?: string }>
85: > {
86:   const [leads, status] = await Promise.all([readLeads(), readStatus()]);
87:   return leads.map((l) => {
88:     const s = status[l.id];
89:     return {
90:       ...l,
91:       status: s?.status ?? "new",
92:       updatedAt: s?.updatedAt,
93:     };
94:   });
95: }
96: 
97: export async function updateLeadStatus(
98:   id: string,
99:   status: LeadStatus,
100:   note?: string,
101: ): Promise<boolean> {
102:   const map = await readStatus();
103:   const prev = map[id];
104:   map[id] = {
105:     status,
106:     updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
107:     note: note ?? prev?.note,
108:   };
109:   await writeStatus(map);
110:   return true;
111: }
112: 
113: export async function getLeadStats(): Promise<{
114:   total: number;
115:   today: number;
116:   thisWeek: number;
117:   byStatus: Record<LeadStatus, number>;
118:   byBudget: Record<string, number>;
119: }> {

121:   const now = Date.now();
122:   const dayMs = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
123: 
124:   const byStatus: Record<LeadStatus, number> = { new: 0, replied: 0, archived: 0 };
125:   const byBudget: Record<string, number> = {};

127:   let today = 0;

132:     if (l.budget) byBudget[l.budget] = (byBudget[l.budget] || 0) + 1;

136:     if (now - t < 7 * dayMs) thisWeek++;
137:   }

140: }

## Summary of files changed

| File | What it does |
|---|---|
| `lib/battle-cards.ts` | Core battle card library — `generateBattleCardHtml()` builds a B&W HTML email template with all required sections (logo wordmark, competitor name, pricing table, top 3 limits, top 3 BAZ strengths, CTA). `sendBattleCard()` is pluggable: `console.log` in dev, drops to Resend API (no new packages) when `RESEND_API_KEY` is set in production. `createBattleCardLead()` stores the lead via the existing `lib/db.ts` with `source="battle_card"` + audit entry. |

Applies rate limiting (5 req/min/IP by key `battle-card-request`) via `lib/rate-limit.ts`, validates all fields, calls `createBattleCardLead()` → `generateBattleCardHtml()` → `sendBattleCard()`, returns `{ ok: true, message: "Battle card sent", leadId }`. |

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `components/marketing/BattleCardForm.tsx` | Updated `onSubmit` to POST to `/api/battle-cards` (instead of `/api/leads`) with the simplified `{ name, email, company, competitor }` payload. |
